# Erik Eremenko — Software Developer (Full Profile)

> Personal website of Erik Eremenko, a Computer Science student at the Technical University of Munich (TUM) with a minor in Mathematics.

## About

Erik Eremenko is a Computer Science student at the Technical University of Munich (minor in Mathematics), currently in his 6th semester. He is passionate about building software that solves real problems — from prediction market platforms to molecular dynamics simulators.

As co-founder of PolySimulator (https://polysimulator.com), he developed a full-stack paper-trading platform that serves 1,880+ registered users. He enjoys working across the stack — from low-level C++ simulations to cloud-native web applications.

He is an early adopter of AI-assisted development workflows, using GitHub Copilot Agent and MCP integrations extensively in his daily work.

## Education

- **Technical University of Munich** — B.Sc. Computer Science, Minor in Mathematics (Oct 2023 – exp. Apr 2027)
- **Königin-Luise-Gymnasium Erfurt** — Abitur, graduated with distinction (1.2/1.0, GPA equiv. 3.85/4.0) (2015 – 2023)

## Featured Projects

### PolySimulator
- Description: AI-powered prediction market paper-trading platform (Polymarket API). 1,880+ registered users, 599 MAU, ~6,270 monthly visitors. Built with AI-agent-assisted development using GitHub Copilot.
- Tech: Python, FastAPI, TypeScript, Next.js, Redis, WebSockets, Supabase, Docker
- Live: https://polysimulator.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/Bavariance

### Molecular Dynamics Simulator
- Description: Bachelor Practical Course at TUM. Group implementation of a molecular dynamics simulator with Lennard-Jones potential and linked-cell algorithm. Performance benchmarking with Valgrind, sanitizers, and systematic testing.
- Tech: C++, CMake, OpenMP, GoogleTest, VTK, Valgrind, Doxygen
- GitHub: https://github.com/ErikEremenko/MolSim-WS25-GroupB

### HackaTUM — IMC Trading Challenge (Bavariance Team)
- Description: Trading bot with Exchange API integration, automated order management and ensemble model for market price prediction. Built during HackaTUM 2025.
- Tech: Python, Exchange API, Algorithmic Trading
- Devpost: https://devpost.com/software/bavariance-imcity
- GitHub: https://github.com/Bavariance/IMCity

### Robo.Innovate Hackathon (TUM Venture Labs — BMW Team A)
- Description: On-premise server setup, training and fine-tuning of deep learning models via imitation learning using the LeRobot framework.
- Tech: Python, Deep Learning, LeRobot, Imitation Learning
- Live: https://unbox-robotics.vercel.app

## Experience

### Co-Founder & Developer — Bavariance (in founding)
- Period: Dec 2025 – Present
- Building PolySimulator, an AI-powered prediction market paper-trading platform. Full-stack development with AI-agent-assisted workflows.

### Bachelor Practical: Molecular Simulation — TU Munich
- Period: Oct 2025 – Feb 2026
- Group implementation of a molecular dynamics simulator. Collaborative PR workflow with mentor reviews and sprint demos.

## Certifications

- **Applied Edge AI: Deep Learning Outside of the Cloud** — Hasso Plattner Institut (Oct 2022) — https://open.hpi.de/verify/xelop-lycuk-copat-ruvin-gypyn
- **Data Science Bootcamp** — Hasso Plattner Institut (Jul 2023) — https://open.hpi.de/verify/xebef-mynal-gosud-kimat-rovov

## Research & Seminars

### Online Machine Learning for Time Series Forecasting
- Venue: Seminar — TU Munich (WS 2025/26)
- Description: Independent seminar paper comparing online learning algorithms for financial time series prediction. Evaluated Online SNARIMAX, Ternary Classifier, and Batch-ARIMA on S&P 500 data using the River framework.
- Topics: Regret Minimization, Online Newton Step, Catastrophic Forgetting, Time Series Forecasting
- Repository: https://github.com/ErikEremenko/online-learning-finance
- Paper (PDF): https://github.com/ErikEremenko/online-learning-finance/blob/main/OL_CAML_TUM_Eremenko_Muller.pdf

### AI & LLM Tools
GitHub Copilot Agent, LLM Dev Workflows, MCP Development, LeRobot

### Programming Languages
Python, C, C++, Java, OCaml, TypeScript, JavaScript

### Web & API
REST API, WebSockets, Node.js, Next.js, Full-Stack

AWS (Lambda, S3, EC2), Cloudflare, Google Cloud, Hetzner, Fly.io, Dokploy

### Databases
Supabase (PostgreSQL), Redis, SQL

### DevOps & Build
Git, Docker, CMake, Bash, CI/CD (GitHub Actions), OpenMP

### Observability
Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, PostHog, MS Clarity

### Math & Data
NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Seaborn

## Spoken Languages
German (native), English (C1), Russian (native), French (DELF B1)
